proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 69
; PROVEN: transitionCount <= num_asserts.
; Model a 2-assert block. Each assertion i yields a binary contribution c_i in {0,1}.
; transitionCount = c1 + c2. The negation (transition_count > num_asserts) is unsat.
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const c1 Int)
(declare-const c2 Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
; Code: num_asserts is a non-negative count of assert lines
(assert (= num_asserts 2))
; Code transitions: each per-assertion contribution is binary (if-body fires or not)
(assert (>= c1 0))
(assert (<= c1 1))
(assert (>= c2 0))
(assert (<= c2 1))
; Code transition: transitionCount is the accumulated sum of contributions
(assert (= transition_count (+ c1 c2)))
; Negation of property: transition_count exceeds the number of assertions
(assert (> transition_count num_asserts))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — c1+c2 <= 1+1 = 2 = num_asserts, so the upper bound holds

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: the early-return `false` when asserts.length === 0
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 69
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: the early-return `false` when asserts.length === 0
; misclassifies a zero-assertion, unconstrained block as non-vacuous.
; Such a block is trivially sat (Z3 returns sat for any variable value),
; which is exactly the vacuous pattern the function is designed to filter.
(declare-const num_declares Int)
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const transition_count Int)
; Code transition: block has at least one declare-const line
(assert (> num_declares 0))
; Code transition: block has zero assert lines → early-return false is taken
(assert (= num_asserts 0))
; Code consequence: the counting loop never executes — transitionCount stays 0
(assert (= transition_count 0))
; isVacuous returns false (early return), but transition_count = 0 and num_asserts = 0
; means the block places no constraints on any variable — it is trivially sat (vacuous)
(assert (= num_declares (+ num_asserts 1)))
(check-sat)
; sat — zero-assertion path is reachable; function incorrectly classifies block as not vacuous
